Petition Against Trustee And Former Co-Trustee For Breaches Of Fiduciary Duty And Breach Of Trust An
Off Calendar Nov-20-2017 Continued To Mar-16-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alan Sparer and Charlotte Ito for Sylvia Donovan.
Jonna Thomas for Margaret Loo.
Konstantine Demiris for Darryl Loo, Clayton Loo, Kaelani Loo and Kyle Loo (Petitioners).

Ms. Thomas stated that Mr. Loo is looking for an eleven year accounting, that she and Mr. Demiris reached a stipulation to continue the evidentiary hearing from December 8, 2017 to a day after February, and that as a matter of law and factual issues she believes the hearing on providing an accounting is still ripe. Mr. Demiris stated that the Trustee had a duty to keep records and account. Mr. Sparer stated that Satsuki's accute illness came about in 2001, raised the issue of needing an evidentiary hearing on Satsuki's capacity and stated that records will not be available any further than 2011 and a little for 2010. The bifurcated issue of the accounting, the Petition for Order Confirming Breach of Trust and Fiduciary Duty, etc., Petition to Approve First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. and Petition to Approve Second Account and Report of Trustee are continued to March 16, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Opening cross briefs are due by March 2, 2018. Cross replies are due by March 8, 2018. The status conference is off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Z. Grimaldi. Not reported.

Petition Against Trustee And Former Co-Trustee For Breaches Of Fiduciary Duty And Breach Of Trust An
Off Calendar Sep-18-2017 Continued To Nov-20-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alan Sparer and Charlotte Ito for Silvia Donovan (Trustee).
Jonna Thomas appeared by telephone for Margaret Loo.
Konstantine Demiris appeared by telephone for Darrell Loo, Clayton Loo, KaeLani Loo and Kyle Loo.

The Parties reported that they are still in the process of conducting discovery, that there is a dispute as to the length of time needed for completion of discovery and the scope of it, that there is an issue with respect to a $26,000.00 loan, whether or not Ms. Loo should be required to account for the period of revocability when one of the Trustees was the Settlor, the $100,000.00 trustee attorney's fees, redaction of e mails, issuance of checks post death, actions during the time the Settlor was in memory care, etc. The Petition Against Trustee and Former Co-Trustee for Breach of Fiduciary Duties and Breach of Trust, the Petition to Approve First Account and Report of Trustee, and the Petition to Approve Second Account and Report of Trustee are continued to November 20, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a status conference. All Counsel are order to be present in Court on that day. Briefs in opposition to the memorandum of points and authorities filed September 13th are due by November 2, 2017. Reply briefs are due by November 17, 2017. An evidentiary hearing on the issue of duties during the period of revocability is set for December 8, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Discovery can remain open. If discovery is objected to, the Court wants the parties to meet and confer and provide letter briefs as to what the scope of discovery is. Discovery requests are due in 10 days (by September 29th) and replies are due five days after that.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Continued To Sep-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition Against Trustee And Former Co-Trustee For Breaches Of Fiduciary Duty And Breach Of Trust An
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Continued To Sep-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alan Sparer and Charlotte Ito for Sylvia Donovan (Trustee).
Jonna Thomas appeared by telephone for Margaret Loo.
Christopher Moore appeared by telephone for Darrell Loo, Clayton Loo, KaeLani Loo and Kyle Loo.

Mr. Sparer stated they are in the process of preparing the real estate for sale, that it is complex, that some of the properties are three condominiums that are in the Mayor's program below market rate, that they are trying to get out of the program and that they are waiting to hear back from the Mayor's Office. Mr. Sparer also stated that there is another property on Church Street that is residential and commercial, that the Trust owns one sixth of the property, that they have asked the other owners if they are interested in buying it and that they are waiting to hear back from them. In addition, Mr. Sparer stated that if they can not agree on a price, they may have to file a partition action, that he just received objections to both of the accountings two days ago, that he would like to reply in writing, that the items in dispute are worth about $50,000.00 and that he would like to figure out a way to resolve the issues without further costs. Furthermore, Mr. Sparer stated that he is willing to do mediation, but he is not optimistic, requested an afternoon to argue the accounting issues, stated that they made investigations as to the prior Trustee, that they produced massive amounts of discovery going back five years before the death of Satsuki, and that he believes they have looked at and identified all of the issues. Mr. Moore stated that all of the issues need to be heard together, that he still needs to do further discovery and take depositions, suggested setting the matter for a three day trial and stated that he believes the accounting issues speak directly as to breach of duties of the Trustee. Ms. Thomas stated that Margaret Loo was a Co-Trustee with the Decedent, that the they never did accountings, that the Decedent never requested an accounting, and argued that there is no legal basis or standing for the Beneficiaries to compel accountings from Margaret Loo. The Court stated that it would like to see replies to the objections in order to get a better idea on how to proceed with the matter. The Petition Against Trustee and Former Co-Trustee for Breach of Fiduciary Duties and Breach of Trust, the Petition to Approve First Account and Report of Trustee, and the Petition to Approve Second Account and Report of Trustee are continued to September 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Replies are due by September 13, 2017.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

The Petition Against Trustee and Former Co-Trustee for Breach of Fiduciary Duty, etc. is continued to July 17, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Petition For Order Determining Title To Property
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Granted Per Written Order

Petition Against Trustee And Former Co-Trustee For Breaches Of Fiduciary Duty And Breach Of Trust An
Off Calendar Nov-14-2016 Continued To Mar-13-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charlotte Ito & Alan Sparer for (trustee) Sylvia Donovan. Konstantine Demiris (via Court Call) for Darryl Loo, Clayton Loo, Kaelani Loo & Kule Loo.

The Court asked about the status of the accounting. Mr. Sparer stated that it was e-filed on Thursday. Mr. Demiris confirmed that he received an email stating that the accounting had been filed. The Court confirmed that there were no objections to the 850 petition and that it will be granted, that there is currently a 2/27/17 deadline for mediation and that it will continue the petition for breach of duty until after that. Mr. Demiris asked if the accounting petition would be set for hearing, that he has no issue with it and asked if the parties could be set for Pro Bono Mediation and confirmed that it qualified. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than February 27, 2017. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 1, 2017. The Petition against former trustee and former co-trustee for breaches of fiduciary duty was continued to and the Parties are to return on March 13, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Mr. Sparer stated that it is possible that the accounting will be assigned for hearing and asked how to take the matter off calendar. The Court instructed Mr. Sparer to talk to the probate examiner if that is the case. The Court granted the petition for order determining title to property (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition Against Trustee And Former Co-Trustee For Breaches Of Fiduciary Duty And Breach Of Trust An
Off Calendar Sep-07-2016 Continued To Nov-14-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Konstantine Demiris (via court call) for (petitioner) Darryl Loo. Charlotte Ito & Allan Sparer for Sylvia Donovan.

The Court asked where things stand and if the parties are open to mediation. Mr. Demiris stated that mediation is a good idea, that before mediation they will need an accounting, that they had requested the information from Mr. Byrne but did not receive complete statements, that in April they were told via email that they would receive the accounting by 5/31/16 and that now Ms. Ito says that an accounting will be done soon. Mr. Sparer stated that the petitioners waived accountings until 3/16/16 and then changed their minds, that Ms. Ito came in shortly after that, that she has been working diligently, that no one promised an accounting for 5/31/16, that they recently obtained valuations on the properties, that most of the issues were delayed over the last few months and are on course, that they do not believe there will be the need for mediation after the accounting is received, that the trustee is serving without compensation and that the family members who took care of the decedent did so without compensation. The Court asked if the end of November is realistic for the accounting, that if it sets a mediation deadline for the end of January would it be sufficient and tried to confirm if this trust qualified for pro bono mediation but the parties are not sure. Mr. Demiris stated that the trust has been in administration for over a year, that they only signed the consent for waiver of accounting because they understood it would be distributed within the year, that they were given 2 promise dates for an accounting and are now being told the end of the year, that they want a neutral professional to prepare the accounting, that there were withdrawals of $75,000.00 by Margaret Loo which was later returned, that there have been significant breaches of trust, that the trustee told his clients via email that they were not entitled to documents and that they cannot allow the accounting to be dragged on any longer. The Court asked even if we put a neutral in place what would be the soonest they could produce an accounting. Mr. Demiris replied that it would take 60 days to marshal the assets and put it into an accounting and that there are various real properties being managed by a property manager. The Court stated that it will set a status on the accounting for November 14, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, that the mediation deadline is set for February 27, 2017 and that between the deadlines they should be able to move along. Mr. Sparer stated that Ms. Ito is working on a problem with title of the properties not in the trust and that she will file a motion. Mr. Demiris asked that if the accounting is not provided by the 11/14/16 date the trustee be suspended. The Court stated that it is not willing to put a suspension in place but if the accounting is not provided it will look at the possibility of a professional fiduciary.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)

Petition Against Trustee And Former Co-Trustee For Breaches Of Fiduciary Duty And Breach Of Trust An
Off Calendar Aug-17-2016 Continued To Sep-07-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
